rent.note: furnished 1BR in Bukit Bintang/KLCC/Mont Kiara; avg $583-630; midpoint

utilities.note: electric + water + fiber internet; Malaysia utilities very affordable

groceries.note: Jaya Grocer / local wet markets; hawker centres cheap

diningOut.note: hawker nasi lemak/char kway teow $1-3; KL food scene diverse and affordable

transit.note: MRT/LRT monthly pass + Grab; KL transport ~$100/mo at comfort level

simData.note: Celcom/Maxis unlimited data RM 50-60/mo; 80-100+ Mbps speeds

misc.note: gym (often free in condo), malls, entertainment, personal care, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers applied to RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Raise for peak tourist/weather months, lower for off-peak.

evidence.claim: KL average nomad monthly ~$1,300; breakdown: accommodation $500, food $350, transport $100, coworking $150, entertainment $200
evidence.quote: The average monthly cost of living in Kuala Lumpur is approximately $1,300 USD, which includes accommodation ($500), food ($350), transport ($100), coworking ($150), and entertainment ($200)

evidence.claim: KL 1BR city center avg $630; rent ~$583/mo average
evidence.quote: average rent for a one-bedroom apartment in the center of Kuala Lumpur costs USD $630. Monthly rent costs are approximately $583 per month

evidence.claim: KL internet consistently 80-100+ Mbps; first-world infrastructure at developing-world prices
evidence.quote: consistently 80-100+ Mbps. The city offers first-world infrastructure at developing-world prices

evidence.claim: KL single person ~$1,122/mo; comfortable nomad $1,750-2,000/mo with lifestyle
evidence.quote: A single person can expect to spend around $1,122 per month. Digital nomads can live comfortably for $1,750–$2,000 per month
